changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: SIGNAL: Iran easing Hormuz restrictions per maritime data — countries cutting individual deals instead of joining US coalition. This is a partial de-escalation on the economic front, though military strikes continue. Europe refuses Hormuz naval deployment. Putin-Trump call today is the key diplomatic variable for Ukraine. Zero ceasefire signals in ME active theaters.

latest_news.headline: Pakistan announces pause in strikes on Afghanistan ahead of Eid al-Fitr
latest_news.text: Pakistan announces temporary pause in strikes against Afghanistan, saying decision made ahead of Muslim holiday Eid al-Fitr. First de-escalation signal since conflict escalated. Underlying issues unresolved.

latest_news.headline: IRGC evacuation order for Gulf energy assets — strikes 'in coming hours'
latest_news.text: IRGC issues evacuation order for all energy assets in Qatar, Saudi Arabia and UAE. 'These facilities have become direct and legitimate targets and will be targeted in the coming hours.' Reuters confirms: Iran listed prominent oil/gas targets as 'direct and legitimate targets.'

latest_news.headline: Qatar condemns South Pars strike as 'dangerous & irresponsible'
latest_news.text: Qatar MFA condemns Israeli targeting of South Pars/North Field facilities as 'dangerous & irresponsible step.' Calls for restraint and de-escalation. Global energy security at risk.

latest_news.headline: IDF strikes Litani River bridges in Lebanon, 2nd division deployed
latest_news.text: IDF begins strikes on bridges across Litani River. Warns all south of Litani to evacuate north. IDF deploys 2nd division to southern Lebanon. 1M+ displaced. Unclear how civilians can evacuate if crossings destroyed.

latest_news.headline: Oil shock: Iran threatens Gulf energy assets, South Pars struck
latest_news.text: Global crude approaches $110/barrel on South Pars strikes + IRGC Gulf evacuation orders. Natural gas prices also spiked. Russia benefits from oil revenue boost.

outlook: CRITICAL (Day 24, 17:04 UTC) — (1) PAKISTAN PAUSES STRIKES on Afghanistan for Eid — first de-escalation signal; (2) IRGC threatens Gulf energy assets 'in coming hours' — Qatar, Saudi, UAE oil/gas infrastructure; (3) South Pars struck, Qatar condemns; (4) IDF hits Litani River bridges, 2nd division in Lebanon; (5) Oil ~$110/barrel. Pakistan-Afghanistan trend shifting down but conflict unresolved. Iran-Gulf remains the hottest front — IRGC explicitly targeting Gulf state energy infrastructure.
